title: Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith (video game)
content:
{{Top|rwm|imp|title=''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith''}} {{Citation}} {{Otheruses|Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith}} {{VideoGame |image=[[File:SWEpisode3gamebox.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars'': Episode III
''Revenge of the Sith'' |developer=[[Wikipedia:The Collective (company)|The Collective]] |publisher=[[LucasArts]] |release date=[[April 2]], [[2005]]{{InsiderCite|Special Edition 2022|Sith Unseen: A Retrospective}} |closed= |upc= |engine= |genre=Action |modes=*Single player *multiplayer |ratings=*[[Wikipedia:Entertainment Software Rating Board|ESRB]] **Teen (T) for PS2 and Xbox **Everyone 10 and Older (E10+) for GBA and Nintendo DS *[[Wikipedia:USK|USK]]: 12 |platforms= *[[Wikipedia:PlayStation 2|PlayStation 2]] *[[Wikipedia:Xbox|Xbox]] *[[Wikipedia:Game Boy Advance|GBA]] *[[Wikipedia:Nintendo DS|Nintendo DS]] *[[Wikipedia:PlayStation 3|PlayStation 3]] *[[Wikipedia:Nintendo GameCube|Nintendo GameCube]] {{C|Cancelled}} *[[Wikipedia:PlayStation Portable|PlayStation Portable]] {{C|Cancelled}} *Mobile phone |series= |basegame= |expansions= |director= |producer= |designer= |programmer= |artist= |writer= |composer= |timeline= |prev= |next= }} {{Quote|At last… a test for my training!|[[Serra Keto]]|''Star Wars Episode III: Revenge of the Sith'' video game}} '''''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith''''' is a 2005 video game based on the [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|movie of the same name]]. It centers on [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] as the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] come to an end. There are seventeen levels, interlaced with over twelve minutes of footage from the movie. It was released on [[May 5]], [[2005]], for the [[Wikipedia:PlayStation 2|PlayStation 2]], [[Wikipedia:Xbox|Xbox]], [[Wikipedia:Game Boy Advance|Game Boy Advance]] and [[Wikipedia:Nintendo DS|Nintendo DS]]. Additionally, a version was made available for mobile phones on [[April 2]], and a version for the [[Wikipedia:PlayStation Portable|PlayStation Portable]] was cancelled and never released. On [[April 28]], [[2015]], the game was released for download on [[Wikipedia:PlayStation Network|PlayStation Network]], and made available to play on [[Wikipedia:PlayStation 3|PlayStation 3]]. Unlike other ''Star Wars'' video games, there was going to be a [[Wikipedia:Nintendo GameCube|Nintendo GameCube]] release, but that one was cancelled for unknown reasons as well. The game was added to [[Wikipedia:Xbox One|Xbox One]] and [[Wikipedia:Xbox Series X and Series S|Xbox Series X/S]] via backwards compatibility on [[November 15]], [[2021]] to celebrate the 20th anniversary of the Xbox. ==Storyline {{SectionFlag|1}}== [[File:Star_Wars_Revenge_of_the_Sith_videogame_Clone_Heavy_Duty_Commandos.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Clone heavy troopers]] The game follows the movie storyline very closely, incorporating footage from the movie and blending it well with the game portion. It involves playing through the events as either [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] or [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], with radically different events taking place in either. As Obi-Wan, much of the game is centered on destroying [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]], while the Anakin levels chronicle his fall to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] and extermination of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. This includes Anakin's attack on the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]], which culminates in a duel with [[Serra Keto]], the best student of [[Cin Drallig/Legends|Cin Drallig]], whom Darth Vader later fights and kills along with Keto. The game ends with the duel on Mustafar between Darth Vader and Obi-Wan. There are two different endings to the final battle, the first being that Obi-Wan defeats Anakin like in the film, the second being that Anakin kills Obi-Wan then meets the Emperor, who gives him a new red [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], Anakin in turn kills the Emperor and claims the new Empire for his own. The [[Wikipedia:Game Boy Advance|Game Boy Advance]] (GBA) and [[Wikipedia:Nintendo DS|Nintendo DS]] versions of ''Episode III'' are much the same as the console versions, except as a side-scrolling action game. The other difference is that while the PS2 and [[Wikipedia:Xbox|Xbox]] versions feature a singular campaign, with the player controlling Obi-Wan or Anakin depending on their scenes in the film, the GBA and DS versions feature two separate campaigns for each one. The DS version also features uses for its touch-screen capability. The game was also released on the mobile phone and followed the storyline of all other versions. It resembles 1999's [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (video game)|''Star Wars: Episode I: The Phantom Menace'' video game]] where the overall game is the same as [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|the corresponding film]] with a few minor changes in exposition and background information. ===Gameplay=== ===Console version=== [[File:ROTS_gameplay_1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Screenshot of the PS2/Xbox version]] The player alternates between playing as Anakin Skywalker and Obi-Wan Kenobi, reliving various scenes from the film through the game's missions. There are 17 levels, interlaced with over 12 minutes of footage from the movie. The game's combat system is heavily concentrated on [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|lightsaber combat]]. Each of the playable characters (with the exception of the MagnaGuard) is equipped with at least one lightsaber. There are three basic attacks: fast attacks, which do the least amount of damage; strong attacks, which do more damage but are slower to execute; and the slowest but strongest critical attacks. These attacks can be mixed to create combination attacks. Attacks can also be charged up for greater strength. One feature of the game is called a Saber Lock, a sequence in which the player's character clashes swords with an opponent. The game features an experience system, whereby the player's character can upgrade attacks and gain new ones as they progress through the game. ===Portable version=== [[File:ROTS_Gameplay_2.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Screenshot of the DS/GBA version]] The handheld versions of the game are played as a 2.5D side-scrolling beat 'em up game, where players can freely choose to play as either Obi-Wan or Anakin, traversing roughly a dozen levels to battle a variety of enemies and occasionally dodge deadly hazards, such as a laser trap or a missile launcher. Both characters have nearly identical attack moves, and can gain access to special unlockable moves that can be unleashed when a "fury" or "focus" gauge is filled up during battle. ===Alternate ending=== In the PS2 and Xbox versions, after completing "Friendship in Flames," one more mission, "Revenge of the Sith," is unlocked. It gives the player the opportunity to play as Anakin (a.k.a. Darth Vader) during the duel on [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]], with the objective of defeating Obi-Wan Kenobi. The duel plays out the same until the very end, where Anakin avoids Obi-Wan's blade in his leap behind Obi-Wan to the lava river bank and fatally [[Shiak|stabs]] Obi-Wan with his lightsaber, killing him. He then kicks Obi-Wan's corpse near the lava. Anakin is then greeted by [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] as he steps out from his shuttle, who tells him, "Excellent work my apprentice. There are none left to oppose us; [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] is ours now." After being presented with a brand-new [[Sith lightsaber/Legends|Sith lightsaber]], Anakin activates his lightsaber and stabs Palpatine. The clone troopers raise their weapons and aim at Anakin for killing the emperor, but do not fire. They lower their weapons as Anakin says, "No... the galaxy belongs to me...!" and serve their new ruler for eternity. ==Characters {{SectionFlag|1}}== ===Playable=== **Enemies-[[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], [[B2-series super battle droid/Legends|B2 super battle droids]] **Allies-[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] (first phase only) Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] **Location-[[Invisible Hand/Legends|''Invisible Hand'']] *The General's Right Hand **Jedi-[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] **Enemies-[[IG-100 MagnaGuard/Legends|IG-100 MagnaGuards]], [[B2 grapple droid/Legends|Grapple droids]], [[Battle droid/Legends|Battle droids]] **Allies-[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] **Location-[[Invisible Hand/Legends|''Invisible Hand'']] *Investigating Utapau **Jedi-[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] **Enemies-[[Battle droid/Legends|Battle droids]], [[Battle droid assassin|droid snipers]], droid]], [[B1-A air battle droid|B1-A Air Battle Droids]] **Allies-None **Location-[[Utapau/Legends|Utapau]] *The Cavalry Arrives **Jedi-[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] **Enemies-[[IG-100 MagnaGuard/Legends|IG-100 MagnaGuards]], [[Battle droid/Legends|Battle droids]], [[Battle droid assassin|droid snipers]], Red [[B2 grapple droid/Legends|B2 Grapple droids]], [[LM-432 crab droid/Legends|LM-432 Crab Droid]], [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] **Allies-[[Clone trooper/Legends|Clone troopers]] **Location-[[Utapau/Legends|Utapau]] *Showdown with Grievous **Jedi-[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] **Enemies-[[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] **Allies-None **Location-[[Utapau/Legends|Utapau]] *The Dark Side of the Force **Jedi-[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] **Enemies-[[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] **Allies-None **Location-[[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] *The Hunt Begins **Sith-[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] **Enemies-[[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]], [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]], [[Jedi Sniper]]s, [[Jedi brute|Jedi Brutes]], [[Eta-2 Actis-class Lesson **Sith-[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] **Enemies-[[Cin Drallig/Legends|Cin Drallig]], [[Serra Keto]] **Allies-None **Location-[[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] *Attack of the Clones **Jedi-[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] **Enemies-[[Clone trooper/Legends|Clone troopers]], [[Heavy weapons clone trooper/Legends|Clone heavy gunner]], [[Clone blaze trooper]]s, [[All Terrain Recon Transport/Legends|AT-RT]], [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry/Legends|LAAT/i]], [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1 battle droids]], [[Droideka/Legends|Droideka]] **Allies-None (although any of the clone troopers can be briefly mind-tricked) **Location-[[Utapau/Legends|Utapau]] *Assassination on Mustafar **Sith-[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] **Enemies-[[Neimoidian Gunnery Battalion|Neimoidian guards]], [[Neimoidian Gunnery Battalion|Neimoidian snipers]], [[Neimoidian Gunnery Battalion|Neimoidian brutes]], [[Sheathipede-class transport shuttle/Legends|Neimoidian shuttle]] with [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] aboard, [[Aruteous Gunnay]], [[Rune Haako/Legends|Rune Haako]], [[Wat Tambor/Legends|Wat Tambor]] **Allies-None **Location-[[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]] *Aftermath in the Temple **Jedi-[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] **Enemies-[[Clone trooper/Legends|Clone troopers]], [[Heavy weapons clone trooper/Legends|Clone heavy gunner]], [[Clone blaze trooper]]s, [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry/Legends|LAAT/i]], [[Clone assassin]]s **Allies-[[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] **Location-[[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] *Friendship in Flames **Jedi-[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] **Enemies-[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] **Allies-None **Location-[[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]] *Revenge of the Sith **Sith-[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] **Enemies-[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] **Allies-None **Location-[[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]] In addition to the main story levels, there are bonus levels and characters the player can unlock and play as, such as [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], General Grievous, an [[IG-100 MagnaGuard/Legends|IG-100 MagnaGuard]] and Darth Vader. Most of these levels consist of the player mainly fighting wave after wave of enemies. An added bonus level is the player taking on the role as Darth Vader during his final [[Rescue of Princess Leia/Legends|encounter]] with Obi-Wan/Ben Kenobi in the original [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']]. ==Multiplayer mode {{SectionFlag|1}}== The game also features a multiplayer [[Lightsaber duel/Legends|duel]] mode, in which two players face off against each other. Players can choose Obi-Wan, Anakin, [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], General Grievous, [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], Cin Drallig, or Serra Keto. Darth Vader and "Ben" Kenobi from Episode IV can also be unlocked for this mode. Each battle can be won with either the best of one, three or five rounds, depending on the options chosen. In addition, all characters have equal health and energy, with all status upgrades acquired by Anakin and Obi-Wan eliminated to balance the fight against the other opponents. However, all of the upgraded techniques and Force powers are available, and each of the other characters has special abilities and maneuvers. In addition to the original costumes found throughout the Single Player campaign, each character has a different costume that is used when each player chooses the same character, so that players can tell their characters apart. Some of these costumes depict [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] versions of certain [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] characters and vice-versa. If one plays Mace Windu fighting Anakin Skywalker on the Death Star, it's sort of implied it's the same as the true film timeline minus Windu and Yoda being the only two living heroes from ''Revenge of the Sith'', Vader never crippled and dying instead at Windu's hand. *Anakin Skywalker: The alternate is hooded and wields a red lightsaber. *Obi-Wan Kenobi: The alternate wears a cape and wields a green lightsaber. *Dooku: The alternate has his [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Attack of the Clones'']] outfit and wields a blue lightsaber. *Grievous: The alternate is slightly tinted blue and wields red lightsabers. *Mace Windu: The alternate has dark clothing and wields a blue lightsaber. *Serra Keto: The alternate has black clothes and wields red lightsabers. *Cin Drallig: The alternate has black clothes and wields a red lightsaber. *Darth Vader: The alternate has lighter armor and wields a blue lightsaber. *Ben Kenobi: The alternate is not hooded and wields a green lightsaber. ==GBA Co-op Mode {{SectionFlag|1}}== In the GBA version, 2 players, with 2 separate consoles and game cartridges, can connect via a Game Boy Advanced Link Cable and play as either Anakin or Obi Wan together in 2 game modes: 'Clone Wars', and 'Last Jedi Standing'. 'Clone Wars' acts as a prologue to the main story, with players fighting against Battle Droids as Anakin and Obi-Wan across 5 separate stages: *[[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]] *[[Muunilinst/Legends|Muunilinst]] *[[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin IV]] *[[Invisible Hand/Legends|Invisible Hand]] I *Invisible Hand II The first 3 stages take clear design inspiration from the [[Battle of Dantooine (Clone Wars)|Battle of Dantooine]], the [[Battle of Muunilinst/Legends|Battle of Muunilinst]], and the [[Duel on Yavin 4|Duel on Yavin IV]], respectively, as shown in ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume One|Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume 1]]''. The Invisible Hand stages reuse the first and second stages of the main story, though with different enemies. Upon first completion, both players are rewarded with upgrade points for the main story for Anakin or Obi-Wan. 'Last Jedi Standing' lets 2 players fights against droids as Anakin and Obi-Wan for as long as possible until either is eliminated. A timer is shown for the time elapsed until one player is eliminated, with the survivor being the winner. Players can choose from 5 maps: *Invisible Hand elevator *Dantooine *Utapau cave *Muunilinst *Yavin IV ==DS starfighter {{SectionFlag|1}}== In the DS version, there are 3D starfighter battles incorporating the touch screen to manage your shields and view radar and scores. There are three missions for each Anakin and Obi-Wan in single player, and a multiplayer providing 8 maps and 17 ships: *[[Eta-2 Actis-class interceptor/Legends|Jedi Interceptor]] *[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] *[[Obi-Wan Kenobi's Eta-2 Actis-class interceptor/Legends|Obi-Wan's Jedi Interceptor]] *[[TIE Advanced x1/Legends|Darth Vader's TIE Advanced]] *[[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighter]] *[[Droid tri-fighter/Legends|Droid tri-fighter]] *[[Alpha-3 Nimbus-class V-wing starfighter/Legends|V-wing]] *[[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]] *[[Nantex-class territorial defense starfighter/Legends|Geonosian Starfighter]] *[[Soulless One/Legends|General Grievous's Belbullab-22 Starfighter]] *[[Mankvim-814 light interceptor/Legends|Techno Union Interceptor]] *[[Azure Angel|Anakin Skywalker's modified Delta-7 Jedi Starfighter]] *[[Anakin Skywalker's Eta-2 Actis-class interceptor/Legends|Anakin's Jedi Interceptor]] *[[Aggressive ReConnaissance-170 starfighter/Legends|ARC-170 starfighter]] *[[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|Vulture Droid Starfighter]] *[[Delta-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|Delta-7 Jedi Starfighter]] ==Music {{SectionFlag|1}}== No original music from the film is used, since recording of the soundtrack had not been completed when the game's production ended. This is also the case in ''[[LEGO Star Wars: The Video Game]]''. As a result, music from all of the other five movies is used. The cutscenes also use music from other media. In the levels "The Final Lesson" and "Assassination on Mustafar," music from ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' is also used. This music can also be found in some multiplayer duel levels. Music from ''Episode III'' was not added in the 2006 "Greatest Hits"/"Platinum Hits" release of the game on PS2 and Xbox. In the GBA and DS versions, music from ''The Phantom Menace'' and ''Attack of the Clones'' was used, although the output slightly varied due to differences in sound output between the two handheld systems. The former utilized a more synthesized track while the latter was generally closer to the music from the film. Both GBA and DS versions also used a limited selection of music from ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume One|Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume 1]]''; notable for being the only official use of the soundtrack from either Volume, having never received an official release. ==Continuity== ===Overall=== Most differences are about character deaths. Because the game was released prior to the movie, many of the important character deaths were changed so as to not spoil the movie. For example: [[File:Dooku-game.jpg|thumb|250px|[[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] in the game]] *The opening cinematic cutscene of the duel with Count Dooku features a line from Dooku that was cut from the film: "Just because there are two of you, do not assume you have the advantage." *Dooku was killed when Anakin impaled him. In the movie, he had his hands and head cut off. This was possibly done for censorship. *When Anakin and Obi-Wan Kenobi fought in the throne room, the dead leaders of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists]] were gone. *Obi-Wan and Grievous ended up in a room overlooking the sinkhole after fighting in a cave, concluding the chase with the General on his wheelbike, being pursued by the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] on [[Boga/Legends|Boga]]. This was instead of the chase concluding on Grievous's landing dock with [[Soulless One/Legends|his starfighter]] as in the movie. *Grievous doesn't cough during gameplay or 3D cutscenes, and he is killed when Obi-Wan stabbed him in the chest, and Grievous's armor short-circuited. In the movie, Obi-Wan used the cyborg general's own blaster to incinerate him with his gut sac exposed after Obi-Wan lost his lightsaber. Owing to this, Obi-Wan in the GBA/DS versions does not say "So uncivilized" in disgust over how he had to kill Grievous. *Mace Windu never fights Palpatine. Instead, Windu duels Anakin and loses, being stabbed through the chest and kicked out the window. The fates of [[Agen Kolar/Legends|Kolar]], [[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Tiin]] and [[Kit Fisto/Legends|Fisto]] are never seen as is the case with several other Jedi. *Cin Drallig was killed with a saber impale variation performed by Darth Vader. In the movie, he was briefly seen being murdered when Vader cut his chest on a recorded hologram seen by Yoda and Obi-Wan. *On his [[Mission to Mustafar/Legends|mission]], Vader kills [[Poggle the Lesser/Legends|Poggle the Lesser]] by using a piece of the door and a [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force push]] to crush him. In the movie, Poggle is cut to pieces. *[[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] escaped Darth Vader's slaughter as opposed to begging for his life prior to being killed. He tried to escape in [[Lapiz Cutter/Legends|his ship]], and then to kill Vader, but Darth Vader destroyed the ship, sending it plummeting into the lava, and then exploding, killing Gunray. *When Darth Vader was defeated on Mustafar, Obi-Wan picked up his lightsaber and left without saying anything. The scene in which Vader is burned alive is also omitted to avoid an M rating from the [[Wikipedia:Entertainment Software Rating Board|ESRB]]. *In the bonus level when Vader confronts Obi-Wan on the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]], Vader is able to cast [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]]. That was not possible because of Vader's mechanical arms. Anakin, in the game, can also use Force lightning as soon as he turned to the dark side, but in the film, he didn't learn that power at all. There are also [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE Interceptors]] in the background. However, that variant of [[TIE series/Legends|TIE fighter]] did not yet exist. [[File:Star_Wars_Revenge_of_the_Sith_videogame_Droidekas_on_Grevious_Separatist_Ship_against_Anakin.jpg|thumb|250px|Anakin is nearly overpowered by an army of Droidekas on the ''Invisible Hand'']] *Palpatine's robe turns from red to black to red again during the fight with Windu. This is because in the movie clips, it is red, but in the game parts, it is black—the same one he wore while captured by Grievous. *Windu and Anakin both wear Jedi robes at the start of their fight, then they lose them, and at the end Anakin has his back on. *[[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]] is seen only in a brief appearance of her head, which is part of a clip from the movie, in the last two levels of the game and is otherwise only mentioned in the PS2 and Xbox versions of the game during the fight on Mustafar. She appears fully in the GBA and DS versions. *Yoda was never shown dueling Palpatine. He is, however, seen taking out more clone troopers in the Jedi temple during the bonus mission where Yoda is a playable character. *Nute Gunray and the [[Separatist Council/Legends|others]] don't know they are being betrayed until Darth Vader arrives in the [[Separatist Council war room/Legends|room]], but in the game they realize Sidious' betrayal as soon as Vader lands, when he kills [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]] guards and fights his way through the whole base to get to the council room. *Since Grievous got stabbed by Obi-Wan in the game he, of course, did not drop his lightsaber like in the movie. Also, in the movie Commander Cody handed Obi-Wan's lightsaber back to Kenobi seconds before Cody received [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] and tells troopers in turrets to fire at Obi-Wan. Instead, Obi-Wan Kenobi tells the clones to continue to higher levels. The clones reply by saying they are no longer required to follow his orders and start firing at the Jedi Master. As this game was made before the [[Inhibitor chip/Legends|inhibitor chip]] [[Retroactive continuity|retcon]], the clone whom Obi-Wan addresses at the start of the level apologizes before firing. *Anakin joins the dark side because he sees Windu attempt to strike down Palpatine, thinking the Jedi are corrupt. In the movie, he joins the dark side because Palpatine says he can help Anakin keep Padmé alive, knowing of the nightmares that Anakin is having. *The in-game dialogue and cinematic cutscenes were written by [[Dark Horse Comics]] assistant editor [[Jeremy Barlow]]. *In the GBA and DS versions there are missions involving Anakin fighting battle droids and an [[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|AAT]] on Coruscant right after he was appointed to the Jedi Council and once again after he decided to rescue Palpatine. Another mission involves Obi-Wan escaping from the clone troopers through the caves on [[Utapau/Legends|Utapau]] and fighting several IG-100 MagnaGuards on the way. There are also some minor differences like the fight with a Vulture droid aboard the [[Invisible Hand/Legends|''Invisible Hand'']] and an escape from the [[IG-227 Hailfire-class droid tank/Legends|''Hailfire''-class droid]] on Mustafar. *Possibly because the game came out first, the music played during the final battle is "[[Duel of the Fates]]," not "[[Battle of the Heroes]]." ===Other versions=== In addition to the above mentioned differences between the movie and the games, there also differences between various versions of the games as well: *The DS version of ''Revenge of the Sith'', which is largely based on the GBA version, includes three space missions where Anakin/Obi-Wan has to destroy some enemies and meet the objectives before moving on to the next mission. Also, the DS version features an exclusive multiplayer mode with only space battles against human players or the AI. The ships can be customized, and some of them have to be unlocked in order to be used in the game. [[File:ROTS_GBA.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Screenshot of the Game Boy Advance version]] *The events of the duel between Anakin and Mace Windu are played similarly to the movie with the GBA and DS versions in comparison to the PS2 and Xbox versions, barring that Anakin strikes Windu before they duel and that Windu is blasted with Force Lightning without being shown hurled out of the window. *The "Ultimate Revenge of the Sith" ending is omitted from the DS and GBA versions. Regardless of whether the player is playing as Anakin/Vader or Obi-Wan, the ultimate outcome of the battle is that Vader is cut down by Obi-Wan. *The soundtrack varies between the GBA and DS versions (specifically, the former is generally more synthesized, while the latter is closer to the soundtrack from the film), most notably with the version of "Duel of the Fates" used: In the former, it was a synthesized version of the theme from the movie, while the latter included vocals, yet was derived more closely from the version from ''Attack of the Clones''. *In the Mustafar missions of the DS version, Nute Gunray and the Separatist leaders are not in the game despite Palpatine's orders given to Anakin to exterminate them. In addition, the duel between Anakin and Obi-Wan Kenobi is much shorter in the GBA and DS versions than in the home console versions. *The lightsaber duels on the handheld consoles are very different from their console counterparts. Each fight begins with the AI executing two different attacks. Each time the player has to block them in order to hit the AI. Every third attack in the beginning of the fight the AI will do a Force push attack, excluding Anakin, Obi-Wan and Grievous. *All of the cutscenes are only featured through images. Some of these feature exclusive deleted scenes such as showing a [[Force ghost/Legends|ghost]] appearance of [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] on [[Polis Massa/Legends|Polis Massa]]. *The levels in the handheld versions are shorter than those on the other consoles due to technical reasons. Some parts are cut or slightly modified in order to accommodate these versions. However, the stories are still told as those on the PS2 and Xbox consoles. ==Credits== {{Credits |cast= *[[James Arnold Taylor]]—[[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] *[[Mat Lucas]]—[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] *[[Terrence Carson]]—[[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] *[[Matthew Wood]]—[[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] *[[Nick Jameson]]—[[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]/Darth Sidious/Ben Kenobi/[[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]] Sniper, Neimoidian Aide *[[Corey Burton]]—[[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]]/[[B1-A air battle droid|Flying Battle Droids]], [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi Sniper]], [[Rune Haako/Legends|Rune Haako]], [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi Brute]], Jedi Pilot *[[Kari Wahlgren]]—[[Serra Keto]] *[[Andrew Chaikin]]—[[Clone trooper/Legends|Clone trooper]]/Clone Captain/[[Cody/Legends|Commander Cody]]/[[Battle droid/Legends|Battle droid]] Commander *[[David W. [[Alethea McGrath]] reprise her role as [[Jocasta Nu/Legends|Jocasta Nu]] while [[Matthew Wood]] reprises his role of General Grievous. This makes Alethea McGrath and Matthew Wood the only actors from the movies to voice their characters in the video-game adaption. |crew= }} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from Star
